main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Dimension Agnostic Neural Process (DANP)","Dimension Aggregator Block (DAB)","Incorporation of Transformer architecture and latent encoding layers"],"problems":["Inability of Neural Process (NP) models to accommodate diverse input and output dimensions across regression tasks","Limited adaptability of learned features in NP models for new tasks with varying dimensions","Need for robust uncertainty-aware meta-learning in settings with limited labeled data"],"datasets":["Various synthetic regression tasks","Various real-world regression tasks"],"metrics":["Predictive performance (e.g., predictive likelihood, accuracy)"],"results":[{"metric":"Predictive performance","value":"DANP achieves notably superior predictive performance compared to previous NP variations"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of Dimension Agnostic Neural Process (DANP) that can handle diverse input and output dimensions in regression tasks","Proposal of the Dimension Aggregator Block (DAB) to transform input features of varying dimensions into a fixed-dimensional representation","Integration of Transformer architecture and latent encoding layers to enhance feature learning and uncertainty modeling in NP models","Demonstration that DANP outperforms previous NP variations on a variety of synthetic and real-world regression tasks"]}

methods: Dimension Agnostic Neural Process (DANP)
Dimension Aggregator Block (DAB)
Incorporation of Transformer architecture and latent encoding layers
problems: Inability of Neural Process (NP) models to accommodate diverse input and output dimensions across regression tasks
Limited adaptability of learned features in NP models for new tasks with varying dimensions
Need for robust uncertainty-aware meta-learning in settings with limited labeled data

results.value: DANP achieves notably superior predictive performance compared to previous NP variations

novelty_claims: Introduction of Dimension Agnostic Neural Process (DANP) that can handle diverse input and output dimensions in regression tasks
Proposal of the Dimension Aggregator Block (DAB) to transform input features of varying dimensions into a fixed-dimensional representation
Integration of Transformer architecture and latent encoding layers to enhance feature learning and uncertainty modeling in NP models
Demonstration that DANP outperforms previous NP variations on a variety of synthetic and real-world regression tasks

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Versatile Neural Processes (VNP) framework","Bottleneck encoder with set tokenizer and self-attention blocks","Hierarchical latent modulated decoder with multiple global latent variables and modulated MLP blocks"],"problems":["Efficient and accurate learning of implicit neural representations (INRs) for complex signals","Overcoming the limited modeling capability and high computational cost of existing Neural Processes (NPs) for complex signals (e.g., high-resolution images, 3D scenes)","Better modeling of global structure and uncertainty in function distributions for INRs"],"datasets":["1D synthetic data","2D images","3D scenes"],"metrics":["Modeling capability (e.g., function approximation accuracy)","Computational cost","Expressiveness for complex signals"],"results":[{"metric":"Modeling capability on 1D synthetic data","value":"Significantly outperforms existing NP approaches"},{"metric":"Accuracy of INRs for 3D scenes","value":"Shows promise in learning accurate INRs without further finetuning"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes Versatile Neural Processes (VNP), a new framework for meta-learning implicit neural representations with high expressiveness for complex signals.","Introduces a bottleneck encoder that produces compact and informative context tokens, reducing computational cost while improving modeling capability.","Designs a hierarchical latent modulated decoder that jointly models global structure and uncertainty using multiple global latent variables, enhancing function approximation for complex signals.","Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on 1D, 2D, and 3D signal tasks, including accurate INR learning for 3D scenes without finetuning."]}

methods: Versatile Neural Processes (VNP) framework
Bottleneck encoder with set tokenizer and self-attention blocks
Hierarchical latent modulated decoder with multiple global latent variables and modulated MLP blocks
problems: Efficient and accurate learning of implicit neural representations (INRs) for complex signals
Overcoming the limited modeling capability and high computational cost of existing Neural Processes (NPs) for complex signals (e.g., high-resolution images, 3D scenes)
Better modeling of global structure and uncertainty in function distributions for INRs

results.value: Significantly outperforms existing NP approaches
Shows promise in learning accurate INRs without further finetuning

novelty_claims: Proposes Versatile Neural Processes (VNP), a new framework for meta-learning implicit neural representations with high expressiveness for complex signals.
Introduces a bottleneck encoder that produces compact and informative context tokens, reducing computational cost while improving modeling capability.
Designs a hierarchical latent modulated decoder that jointly models global structure and uncertainty using multiple global latent variables, enhancing function approximation for complex signals.
Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on 1D, 2D, and 3D signal tasks, including accurate INR learning for 3D scenes without finetuning.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Latent Bottlenecked Attentive Neural Processes (LBANP)","Encoding context dataset into a fixed number of latent vectors with self-attention","Multiple cross-attention mechanisms on latent vectors for prediction","Variants of LBANP for different settings"],"problems":["Scalability of Neural Processes (NPs) with respect to the number of context datapoints","High computational cost (quadratic complexity) of attention-based NP variants like Transformer Neural Processes (TNPs)","Poor performance of existing sub-quadratic NP variants compared to TNPs","Inapplicability of existing methods to large datasets due to computational constraints"],"datasets":["Meta-regression datasets","Image completion datasets","Contextual multi-armed bandit datasets"],"metrics":["Predictive performance (e.g., accuracy, RMSE, or similar)","Computational complexity (Big-O notation)","Scalability to large datasets"],"results":[{"metric":"Predictive performance","value":"Competitive with state-of-the-art on meta-regression, image completion, and contextual multi-armed bandits"},{"metric":"Computational complexity (query step)","value":"Independent of the number of context datapoints; linear in number of latent vectors"},{"metric":"Scalability","value":"LBANPs can scale to larger dataset settings where existing attention-based NP variants fail"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes LBANP, a new sub-quadratic NP variant with querying computational complexity independent of the number of context datapoints","LBANP encodes the context dataset into a constant number of latent vectors, enabling efficient self-attention and cross-attention mechanisms","LBANP achieves a trade-off between computational cost and performance via the number of latent vectors","LBANP can scale to larger datasets than previous attention-based NP variants","LBANP achieves competitive results with state-of-the-art methods while being more computationally efficient"]}

methods: Latent Bottlenecked Attentive Neural Processes (LBANP)
Encoding context dataset into a fixed number of latent vectors with self-attention
Multiple cross-attention mechanisms on latent vectors for prediction
Variants of LBANP for different settings
problems: Scalability of Neural Processes (NPs) with respect to the number of context datapoints
High computational cost (quadratic complexity) of attention-based NP variants like Transformer Neural Processes (TNPs)
Poor performance of existing sub-quadratic NP variants compared to TNPs
Inapplicability of existing methods to large datasets due to computational constraints
metrics: Predictive performance (e.g., accuracy, RMSE, or similar)

results.value: Competitive with state-of-the-art on meta-regression, image completion, and contextual multi-armed bandits
Independent of the number of context datapoints; linear in number of latent vectors
LBANPs can scale to larger dataset settings where existing attention-based NP variants fail

novelty_claims: Proposes LBANP, a new sub-quadratic NP variant with querying computational complexity independent of the number of context datapoints
LBANP encodes the context dataset into a constant number of latent vectors, enabling efficient self-attention and cross-attention mechanisms
LBANP achieves a trade-off between computational cost and performance via the number of latent vectors
LBANP can scale to larger datasets than previous attention-based NP variants
LBANP achieves competitive results with state-of-the-art methods while being more computationally efficient

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Self-normalized Importance weighted Neural Process (SI-NP)","Surrogate objective for target log-likelihood within the expectation maximization framework","Analysis of optimization objectives for neural processes (e.g., ELBOs, Monte Carlo log-likelihood estimates)"],"problems":["Inference suboptimality in vanilla Neural Processes (NPs)","Underfitting and suboptimal performance of NPs","Lack of analysis of NP optimization objectives and their impact on learned functional priors"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Target log-likelihood","Meta-dataset likelihood"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a surrogate objective for the target log-likelihood of the meta dataset within the expectation maximization framework for NPs.","Introduces the Self-normalized Importance weighted Neural Process (SI-NP) that can learn a more accurate functional prior and has an improvement guarantee concerning the target log-likelihood.","Provides an analysis of the inference suboptimality of vanilla NPs from the perspective of optimization objectives, which has not been sufficiently investigated in prior work.","Demonstrates the equivalence of conditional NPs and SI-NPs with one Monte Carlo sample estimate, relating to prior collapse.","Shows that SI-NPs can be augmented with structural inductive biases (e.g., attention modules) to achieve state-of-the-art performance."]}

methods: Self-normalized Importance weighted Neural Process (SI-NP)
Surrogate objective for target log-likelihood within the expectation maximization framework
Analysis of optimization objectives for neural processes (e.g., ELBOs, Monte Carlo log-likelihood estimates)
problems: Inference suboptimality in vanilla Neural Processes (NPs)
Underfitting and suboptimal performance of NPs
Lack of analysis of NP optimization objectives and their impact on learned functional priors
novelty_claims: Proposes a surrogate objective for the target log-likelihood of the meta dataset within the expectation maximization framework for NPs.
Introduces the Self-normalized Importance weighted Neural Process (SI-NP) that can learn a more accurate functional prior and has an improvement guarantee concerning the target log-likelihood.
Provides an analysis of the inference suboptimality of vanilla NPs from the perspective of optimization objectives, which has not been sufficiently investigated in prior work.
Demonstrates the equivalence of conditional NPs and SI-NPs with one Monte Carlo sample estimate, relating to prior collapse.
Shows that SI-NPs can be augmented with structural inductive biases (e.g., attention modules) to achieve state-of-the-art performance.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Transformer Neural Processes (TNPs): a transformer-based neural process model for uncertainty-aware meta learning using autoregressive sequence and Bayesian optimization"}],"novelty_claims":["Casting uncertainty-aware meta learning as a sequence modeling problem using transformers","Autoregressive likelihood-based training objective that removes the need for latent variables and variational approximations","Transformer-based architecture with modifications for invariance and equivariance to context/target points","Monte Carlo symmetrization for equivariant predictive distributions","Variants of TNPs that trade off expressivity and computational tractability via covariance approximations"]}

methods: Transformer Neural Processes (TNPs): a transformer-based neural process model for uncertainty-aware meta learning using autoregressive sequence modeling
Autoregressive likelihood-based training objective for meta learning
Transformer architecture with causal masking, removal of positional embeddings, and novel padding/masking for context/target points
Monte Carlo approximation to a symmetrized predictive distribution for equivariance
Two TNP variants: diagonal and Cholesky approximations to the covariance matrix for output distribution
problems: Underfitting and intractable likelihoods in Neural Processes (NPs) for meta learning
Poor uncertainty estimation and overconfident predictions in Attentive Neural Processes (ANPs)
Lack of invariance and equivariance in transformer-based models for meta learning
Improving performance on sequential decision making tasks (e.g., contextual bandits, Bayesian optimization)
datasets: Meta regression benchmarks (as in prior NP works)
Image completion benchmarks (as in prior NP works)
metrics: Prediction quality (e.g., RMSE, NLL, as used in meta regression/image completion)
Performance on sequential decision making tasks (e.g., cumulative reward, regret)

results.value: TNPs outperform all previous NP variants on meta regression, image completion, contextual multi-armed bandits, and Bayesian optimization

novelty_claims: Casting uncertainty-aware meta learning as a sequence modeling problem using transformers
Autoregressive likelihood-based training objective that removes the need for latent variables and variational approximations
Transformer-based architecture with modifications for invariance and equivariance to context/target points
Monte Carlo symmetrization for equivariant predictive distributions
Variants of TNPs that trade off expressivity and computational tractability via covariance approximations

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["UNLIMITD (uncertainty-aware meta-learning for multimodal task distributions)","UNLIMITD-I (variant)","UNLIMITD-R (variant)","UNLIMITD-F (variant)","Bayesian inference on a linearized neural network","Gaussian process theory for constructing task distributions"],"problems":["Meta-learning with limited context data (few-shot learning)","Uncertainty estimation in meta-learning","Detection of out-of-distribution (OoD) tasks in meta-learning","Meta-learning on heterogeneous, multimodal task distributions"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes UNLIMITD, a novel uncertainty-aware meta-learning method for multimodal task distributions.","UNLIMITD models the true distribution of tasks with a learnable distribution constructed over a linearized neural network using analytic Bayesian inference.","Introduces three variants (UNLIMITD-I, UNLIMITD-R, UNLIMITD-F) to balance prior richness and network expressivity.","Demonstrates efficient probabilistic predictions and OoD detection in both unimodal and multimodal task settings."]}

methods: UNLIMITD (uncertainty-aware meta-learning for multimodal task distributions)
UNLIMITD-I (variant)
UNLIMITD-R (variant)
UNLIMITD-F (variant)
Bayesian inference on a linearized neural network
Gaussian process theory for constructing task distributions
problems: Meta-learning with limited context data (few-shot learning)
Uncertainty estimation in meta-learning
Detection of out-of-distribution (OoD) tasks in meta-learning
Meta-learning on heterogeneous, multimodal task distributions
novelty_claims: Proposes UNLIMITD, a novel uncertainty-aware meta-learning method for multimodal task distributions.
UNLIMITD models the true distribution of tasks with a learnable distribution constructed over a linearized neural network using analytic Bayesian inference.
Introduces three variants (UNLIMITD-I, UNLIMITD-R, UNLIMITD-F) to balance prior richness and network expressivity.
Demonstrates efficient probabilistic predictions and OoD detection in both unimodal and multimodal task settings.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["NP-Match: a semi-supervised learning method based on neural processes (NPs) for image classification","Incorporation of neural processes (NPs) into deep neural networks for probabilistic modeling in SSL","Combination of consistency regularization and pseudo-labeling within the NP-Match framework","Proposal of a new uncertainty-guided skew-geometric Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence for training NP-Match"],"problems":["Semi-supervised image classification: leveraging unlabeled data to reduce reliance on labeled data","Efficient and effective uncertainty estimation in semi-supervised learning","Overcoming the computational inefficiency of MC dropout-based probabilistic SSL methods","Improving the quality of pseudo-labels for unlabeled data in SSL"],"datasets":["CIFAR-10","CIFAR-100","SVHN","mini-ImageNet"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy","Training and inference time (efficiency)"],"results":[{"metric":"Classification accuracy (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, mini-ImageNet)","value":"NP-Match outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) results or achieves competitive results on all four datasets"},{"metric":"Uncertainty estimation efficiency","value":"NP-Match estimates uncertainty faster than MC-dropout-based probabilistic models, improving both training and test efficiency"}],"novelty_claims":["First work to apply neural processes (NPs) to large-scale semi-supervised image classification","NP-Match is a new probabilistic SSL method that can break the monopoly of MC dropout as the probabilistic model in SSL","Proposal of a new uncertainty-guided skew-geometric Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence for neural processes in SSL, addressing the limitations of KL divergence in this context","NP-Match achieves better or competitive performance compared to SOTA methods while being more computationally efficient for uncertainty estimation"]}

methods: NP-Match: a semi-supervised learning method based on neural processes (NPs) for image classification
Incorporation of neural processes (NPs) into deep neural networks for probabilistic modeling in SSL
Combination of consistency regularization and pseudo-labeling within the NP-Match framework
Proposal of a new uncertainty-guided skew-geometric Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence for training NP-Match
problems: Semi-supervised image classification: leveraging unlabeled data to reduce reliance on labeled data
Efficient and effective uncertainty estimation in semi-supervised learning
Overcoming the computational inefficiency of MC dropout-based probabilistic SSL methods
Improving the quality of pseudo-labels for unlabeled data in SSL

results.value: NP-Match outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) results or achieves competitive results on all four datasets
NP-Match estimates uncertainty faster than MC-dropout-based probabilistic models, improving both training and test efficiency

novelty_claims: First work to apply neural processes (NPs) to large-scale semi-supervised image classification
NP-Match is a new probabilistic SSL method that can break the monopoly of MC dropout as the probabilistic model in SSL
Proposal of a new uncertainty-guided skew-geometric Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence for neural processes in SSL, addressing the limitations of KL divergence in this context
NP-Match achieves better or competitive performance compared to SOTA methods while being more computationally efficient for uncertainty estimation

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Autoregressive Conditional Neural Processes (AR CNPs): deploying CNPs in autoregressive mode at test time to model dependencies without changing the model or training procedure","Multiscale architecture for ConvCNPs to handle high-resolution climate data","Combining AR ConvCNPs with a beta-categorical mixture likelihood"],"problems":["Inability of standard Conditional Neural Processes (CNPs) to model dependencies in their predictions","Limitations of existing solutions that require approximate inference or are restricted to Gaussian predictions","Need for efficient and tractable methods to model non-Gaussian, dependent predictive distributions in meta-learning and regression tasks"],"datasets":["Synthetic regression tasks (Gaussian and non-Gaussian)","Real-world climate data"],"metrics":["Predictive log-likelihood"],"results":[{"metric":"Predictive log-likelihood","value":"AR CNPs are consistently competitive with, and often significantly outperform, all other neural process models in terms of predictive log-likelihood across a range of Gaussian and non-Gaussian regression tasks"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposing the use of autoregressive sampling at test time for CNPs to model dependencies without modifying the model or training procedure","Demonstrating that AR CNPs can capture rich, non-Gaussian predictive distributions and produce coherent samples, despite being trained with Gaussian likelihoods and not explicitly modeling dependencies","Proving that AR CNPs can match or exceed the performance of models that explicitly model correlations, given sufficient data and capacity","Introducing a multiscale architecture for ConvCNPs to handle high-resolution climate data","Combining AR ConvCNPs with a beta-categorical mixture likelihood for strong performance on real-world tasks"]}

methods: Autoregressive Conditional Neural Processes (AR CNPs): deploying CNPs in autoregressive mode at test time to model dependencies without changing the model or training procedure
Multiscale architecture for ConvCNPs to handle high-resolution climate data
Combining AR ConvCNPs with a beta-categorical mixture likelihood
problems: Inability of standard Conditional Neural Processes (CNPs) to model dependencies in their predictions
Limitations of existing solutions that require approximate inference or are restricted to Gaussian predictions
Need for efficient and tractable methods to model non-Gaussian, dependent predictive distributions in meta-learning and regression tasks

results.value: AR CNPs are consistently competitive with, and often significantly outperform, all other neural process models in terms of predictive log-likelihood across a range of Gaussian and non-Gaussian regression tasks

novelty_claims: Proposing the use of autoregressive sampling at test time for CNPs to model dependencies without modifying the model or training procedure

Demonstrating that AR CNPs can capture rich, non-Gaussian predictive distributions and produce coherent samples, despite being trained with Gaussian likelihoods and not explicitly modeling dependencies
Proving that AR CNPs can match or exceed the performance of models that explicitly model correlations, given sufficient data and capacity
Introducing a multiscale architecture for ConvCNPs to handle high-resolution climate data
Combining AR ConvCNPs with a beta-categorical mixture likelihood for strong performance on real-world tasks

methods: Bootstrapping Neural Process (BNP): an extension of Neural Processes (NP) using the bootstrap to induce functional uncertainty

problems: Limited flexibility of Neural Processes (NPs) due to modeling uncertainty with a single latent variable

Desire for minimal modeling assumptions in learning stochastic processes from data
novelty_claims: Proposes Bootstrapping Neural Process (BNP), a novel extension of NP using the bootstrap for functional uncertainty
BNP induces functional uncertainty without assuming a particular form, relying on data-driven bootstrap resampling
BNP can be applied to any NP variant with minimal additional parameters
BNP demonstrates improved robustness, especially under model-data mismatch, compared to existing NP models with global latent variables

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Attentive Neural Processes (ANPs): Neural Processes augmented with differentiable attention mechanisms to allow each input location to attend to relevant context points for prediction"],"problems":["Underfitting of context points in Neural Processes (NPs) leading to inaccurate predictions at observed data inputs","Limited expressiveness and reconstruction accuracy of standard NPs in regression tasks"],"datasets":["1D function regression datasets (synthetic)","2D image regression datasets (e.g., face images, as in the top/bottom half prediction task)"],"metrics":["Prediction accuracy at context points","Reconstruction error","Training speed (iterations and wall clock time)"],"results":[{"metric":"Prediction accuracy at context points (qualitative)","value":"ANPs provide noticeably more accurate predictions than NPs at context points in both 1D and 2D regression tasks"},{"metric":"Training speed","value":"ANPs train noticeably faster than NPs, both in terms of iterations and wall clock time"}],"novelty_claims":["Incorporation of differentiable attention into Neural Processes to address underfitting and improve prediction accuracy at context points","Demonstration that attention-based aggregation allows the model to focus on relevant context points for each prediction, overcoming the bottleneck of mean-aggregation in standard NPs","Empirical evidence that ANPs expand the range of functions that can be modeled and improve both reconstruction and training speed over standard NPs"]}

methods: Attentive Neural Processes (ANPs): Neural Processes augmented with differentiable attention mechanisms to allow each input location to attend to relevant context points for prediction
problems: Underfitting of context points in Neural Processes (NPs) leading to inaccurate predictions at observed data inputs
Limited expressiveness and reconstruction accuracy of standard NPs in regression tasks
datasets: 2D image regression datasets (e.g., face images, as in the top/bottom half prediction task)
metrics: Training speed (iterations and wall clock time)

results.value: ANPs provide noticeably more accurate predictions than NPs at context points in both 1D and 2D regression tasks
ANPs train noticeably faster than NPs, both in terms of iterations and wall clock time

novelty_claims: Incorporation of differentiable attention into Neural Processes to address underfitting and improve prediction accuracy at context points
Demonstration that attention-based aggregation allows the model to focus on relevant context points for each prediction, overcoming the bottleneck of mean-aggregation in standard NPs
Empirical evidence that ANPs expand the range of functions that can be modeled and improve both reconstruction and training speed over standard NPs

methods: Neural Processes for Continual Learning (NPCL): a hierarchical latent variable model with global and task-specific latent variables
Regularization of latent variables to mitigate forgetting by keeping them close to their old forms
Use of entropy as an uncertainty quantification metric for task head/module inference

Lack of reliable uncertainty estimation in continual learning models

novelty_claims: First use of neural processes (NPs) for continual learning, leveraging their probabilistic nature for uncertainty estimation.
Introduction of a hierarchical latent variable model (NPCL) with both global and task-specific latents for better knowledge transfer and reduced forgetting.
Proposal of regularizing latent variables (rather than parameters) to mitigate forgetting, showing advantages over parameter-based regularization.
Demonstration that NPCL's uncertainty estimation can be used for task head inference, novel data detection, and instance-level confidence evaluation.
NPCL achieves better or comparable results to state-of-the-art deterministic continual learning methods, which are typically more accurate than probabilistic models.

methods: Gaussian Neural Processes (GNPs): a class of models that directly parameterize the covariance of a Gaussian predictive process, enabling tractable dependent predictions and analytic likelihoods
ConvGNP: a specific architecture within GNPs that uses convolutional neural networks to model predictive covariances efficiently
Composition with invertible marginal transformations to model non-Gaussian output distributions
problems: CNPs do not produce correlated (dependent) predictions, making them unsuitable for tasks requiring coherent function samples (e.g., environmental or healthcare predictions)
Existing models that capture dependencies (e.g., NPs, FullConvGNP) are either hard to train or computationally expensive, especially for high-dimensional data
Need for models that provide dependent predictions, are simple to train, computationally tractable, and applicable to real-world estimation tasks
datasets: Synthetic data drawn from Gaussian Processes with various covariances (exponentiated quadratic, Matern, noisy mixture, weakly periodic)
Climate data (for statistical downscaling and temperature prediction)
metrics: Predictive performance (e.g., log-likelihood, RMSE, or similar, though not explicitly named in the excerpt)
Ability to produce spatially coherent samples

results.metric: Predictive performance on synthetic and real data
results.value: GNPs outperform mean-field and latent variable models

results.metric: Performance on EEG and climate data
results.value: GNPs outperform mean-field and latent variable counterparts
GNPs outperform a standard ensemble of widely used methods and provide spatially coherent temperature samples

novelty_claims: Introduction of Gaussian Neural Processes (GNPs), which directly parameterize the covariance of the predictive process, allowing for tractable dependent predictions and analytic likelihoods
GNPs circumvent the computational cost of FullConvGNPs and are applicable to higher-dimensional input data
GNPs can be composed with invertible marginal transformations to model non-Gaussian data
Demonstration that modelling correlations improves performance on both synthetic and real-world tasks, including tasks that mean-field models cannot solve
GNPs provide spatially coherent samples necessary for climate impact studies, outperforming standard methods in statistical downscaling

methods: Relational Conditional Neural Processes (RCNPs)
Relational encoding for equivariances in neural process models
Two variants of relational encoding: 'Full' and 'Simple'

Posterior predictive inference using local latent variables and reference sets
Scalable training via mini-batch optimization

methods: Uncertainty-Aware Edge Detector (UAED)
Modeling label ambiguity as a learnable Gaussian distribution (mean and variance)
Adaptive weighting loss that emphasizes learning from pixels with high uncertainty
Encoder-decoder architecture with two decoders (one for mean, one for variance)
problems: Edge detection with multiple, subjective, and ambiguous annotations

Ineffective use of hard samples (pixels with high uncertainty) in training

novelty_claims: First work to provide an uncertainty perspective in edge detection by modeling label ambiguity from multiple annotations as a learnable distribution.
Design of an adaptive weighting loss that leverages uncertainty to focus training on hard samples (pixels with high uncertainty), rather than discarding them.
UAED can be combined with various encoder-decoder backbones with minimal computational overhead and achieves consistent improvement across datasets and architectures.

methods: Uncertainty-aware Distillation with Class Equilibrium (UaD-CE) framework
Uncertainty-aware Distillation (UaD) module
Class Equilibrium (CE) module
Class-balanced self-training (CB_ST)
Uncertainty-guided knowledge refinement
Adaptive distillation
problems: Few-shot class-incremental learning (FSCIL) with limited labeled data
Overfitting on novel categories in FSCIL
Catastrophic forgetting of old categories in FSCIL
Classification bias towards base classes due to data imbalance
Adaptability issue of semi-supervised learning for FSCIL (Semi-FSCIL)
Effective utilization of unlabeled data in FSCIL
novelty_claims: First to focus on the adaptability issue of semi-supervised learning for FSCIL and explore the upper bound of performance improvement with unlabeled data.
Proposes a unified framework (UaD-CE) that combines uncertainty-aware distillation and class equilibrium for Semi-FSCIL.
Introduces a class-balanced self-training (CB_ST) approach to avoid dominance of easy-to-classified classes in pseudo-label generation.
Implements uncertainty-guided knowledge refinement for more effective adaptive distillation, improving knowledge transfer and reducing ambiguity between old and new categories.
Provides comprehensive assessment on three benchmark datasets, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.
